title: Prunus pedunculata
taxonomy: Prunus pedunculata is a species of flowering plant, and it belongs to the family Rosaceae. The genus Prunus is a group of flowering plants that includes cherries, plums, and peaches. Prunus pedunculata is further classified into the subfamily Amygdaloideae, which is a subfamily of the Rosaceae family. This subfamily includes stone fruits, such as apricots and plums. Prunus pedunculata is native to specific regions and has unique characteristics that distinguish it from other species within the genus.
uses: Prunus pedunculata is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, but information regarding its medicinal uses is limited. benefits: Prunus pedunculata, also known as the Western chokecherry, is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine.
